items.title: NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 Brings Advanced Reasoning To Physical AI
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet: The Hugging Face blog in early 2026 has seen a wave of new model releases across multiple organizations. NVIDIA has been particularly active, releasing Cosmos Reason 2 — an advanced reasoning vision-language model that tops the Physical AI Bench leaderboard — alongside the Nemotron 3 Nano series, a new standard for efficient agentic models, and Llama Nemotron VL-1B multimodal embedding models for RAG pipelines. The blog also featured the release of Falcon-H1-Arabic from TII UAE, pushing boundari

items.title: Cursor: The best way to code with AI
items.author: cursor.com
items.snippet:
Cursor has seen significant updates in early 2026, including the launch of self-hosted cloud agents (March 25, 2026) that keep code and tool execution entirely within a user's own network. Composer 2 became available on March 19, 2026, offering frontier-level coding performance. Cursor also expanded its plugin ecosystem with 30+ new integrations from partners like Atlassian, Datadog, GitLab, Hugging Face, and PlanetScale.

Windsurf (Codeium) has been rapidly expanding model support, adding GPT-5

items.title: LangChain - Changelog | LangGraph 1.0 is now generally available
items.author: changelog.langchain.com
items.snippet: As of March 2026, the AI agent framework landscape has seen significant consolidation and major version milestones. LangGraph reached v1.0 GA in October 2025 and is now at v1.1.2 (released March 12, 2026), with recent additions including type-safe streaming via a new `version="v2"` API, typed invoke returning structured `GraphOutput` objects, and fixes for time-travel with interrupts. The npm package `@langchain/langgraph` is at v1.2.5 as of late March 2026, reflecting rapid iteration. LangGraph

items.title: Introducing the First Frontier Suite built on Intelligence + Trust
items.author: blogs.microsoft.com
items.snippet: Microsoft has been actively expanding its Copilot AI offerings in early 2026. In March 2026, Microsoft announced the 'Frontier Suite' — a new Microsoft 365 E7 tier at $99/user/month — bundling Wave 3 of Microsoft 365 Copilot with expanded model diversity, including Claude and next-generation OpenAI models. Agent 365, a new standalone agentic product, was announced for general availability at $15/user/month starting May 1, 2026. Wave 3 brings next-generation agentic experiences across Word, Excel

items.title: Hugging Face Opens GitHub Copilot Chat to Open-Source Models
items.author: devops.com
items.snippet: The open source AI model ecosystem has seen explosive growth heading into spring 2026. Hugging Face reports reaching 13 million users, over 2 million public models, and 500,000+ public datasets — nearly doubling year-over-year. A major driver has been the proliferation of Chinese open-weight models following DeepSeek R1's viral January 2025 release, prompting Western organizations to accelerate competitive open alternatives including OpenAI's GPT-OSS, AI2's OLMo, and Google's Gemma. South Korean

items.snippet: Meta's most significant recent open source AI release is the Llama 4 model family, announced in early April 2025. items.title: Open LLM Leaderboard - Hugging Face Space
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet: As of March 2026, the open source LLM landscape is highly competitive, with several new models emerging that rival or surpass proprietary systems on key benchmarks. The Hugging Face Open LLM Leaderboard tracks performance across IFEval, BBH, MATH, GPQA, MUSR, and MMLU-PRO. S-tier open models include GLM-4.7, Kimi K2.5, and MiniMax M2.5 — Kimi K2.5 leads on HumanEval (99.0) and MATH-500, while GLM-4.7 scores 94.2 on HumanEval, 95.7 on AIME 2025, 85.7 on GPQA Diamond, and offers a 200K context win

items.title: A Survey of AI Agent Protocols
items.author: arxiv.org
items.snippet: This week on arXiv, research on LLM-based agents spans planning, market dynamics, and learning from experience. A new AI Planning Framework for LLM-Based Web Agents (2603.12710) maps modern agent architectures to classical search paradigms — step-by-step agents to BFS, tree-search agents to best-first search, and full-plan-in-advance agents to DFS, providing a rigorous taxonomy for web task automation. Separately, Retrieval-Augmented LLM Agents: Learning to Learn from Experience (2603.18272) pro

items.title: New Privacy, Data Protection and AI Laws in 2026 | Pearl Cohen
items.author: www.pearlcohen.com
items.snippet: The EU AI Act continues its phased rollout, with full applicability set for August 2, 2026, covering high-risk AI systems across member states. However, the European Commission is reportedly considering a one-year delay for high-risk system obligations amid industry pressure and readiness concerns. The transparency and GPAI model rules already took effect in August 2025, and prohibited AI practices have been enforced since February 2025. In the US, a December 2025 Executive Order titled 'Ensurin

items.title: Unpacking the December 11, 2025 Executive Order on AI | Sidley Data Matters
items.author: datamatters.sidley.com
items.snippet: The EU AI Act continues its phased rollout, with full applicability set for August 2, 2026, covering high-risk AI systems across member states. However, the European Commission is reportedly considering a one-year delay for high-risk system obligations amid industry pressure and readiness concerns. The transparency and GPAI model rules already took effect in August 2025, and prohibited AI practices have been enforced since February 2025. In the US, a December 2025 Executive Order titled 'Ensurin

items.title: Designing Agentic AI-Based Screening for Portfolio Investment
items.author: arxiv.org
items.snippet: This week on arXiv, research on LLM-based agents spans planning, market dynamics, and learning from experience. A new AI Planning Framework for LLM-Based Web Agents (2603.12710) maps modern agent architectures to classical search paradigms — step-by-step agents to BFS, tree-search agents to best-first search, and full-plan-in-advance agents to DFS, providing a rigorous taxonomy for web task automation. Separately, Retrieval-Augmented LLM Agents: Learning to Learn from Experience (2603.18272) pro
